title: Haunting Events
description: Paranormal occurrences in a location — the specific, concrete events that confirm something is wrong with this place.

entries.result: A door opens by itself, slowly, to reveal nothing on the other side.
A figure appears in the window of an empty room, visible from outside.
A voice — clear, close, identifiable as a specific person — calls someone's name from an empty part of the building.
All the lights extinguish simultaneously and return after a count of three.
An object rises from a surface, hangs for a moment, and falls.
A figure is seen in a mirror. When the observer turns, no one is behind them.
Handwriting appears on a fogged surface, addressed to someone in the room by name.
A previously locked room is found arranged differently — furniture moved, objects placed with deliberate intent.
Everyone in the building hears a child crying from the basement. There are no children.
A cold hand touches someone's shoulder. There is no one behind them.
A figure is seen at the end of the hallway that is not there when the group reaches it.
An old music box begins playing. It is wound down. It has no mechanism for starting itself.
A voice answers a question one of the party asked aloud, not expecting a response.
All the doors in the building slam simultaneously.
A child appears in a doorway, looks at the party, and walks away. Following reveals nothing.
An old photograph on a shelf moves — subtly — to face one of the party directly.
Blood drips from the ceiling with no source above and no damage to the structure.
A figure stands outside the window in the rain. When a light is turned on, it is gone. The rain continues.
Books fall from a shelf in sequence, specific books, as if selected.
A recording device captures something that was not audible at the time of recording.
The temperature of one specific room drops precipitously when a particular topic is spoken about.
A phone rings. On answering, the caller is speaking in a voice the recipient recognizes as someone dead.
Every mirror in the building shows a reflection that is slightly delayed.
An imprint appears on an empty bed — the shape of a reclining figure, forming while the party watches.
A figure seen repeatedly in different locations of the building — same posture, same position, never moving.
A stain appears on the ceiling that takes the shape of a human outline.
An old radio turns itself on and plays a specific song.
A candle flame bends toward a specific doorway despite the absence of any air current.
Something knocks from inside the closet. Three knocks. When opened: nothing, but the air inside is warm.
An object from a locked case is found placed in the center of a room, standing upright.
The party splits up; each member separately reports seeing the same figure in their section of the building.
A photograph develops new content when examined — a face in a window that wasn't visible originally.
The sound of a typewriter from an empty room — stopping the moment the door is touched.
Frost forms on the inside of a window in a pattern that suggests a face.
An attic accessed for the first time in years shows evidence of recent habitation — a bed, a lamp, food.
A specific section of floor feels wrong to walk on — not structurally weak, simply wrong.
Every clock in the building begins moving backward at the same moment.
A figure stands in the center of the basement looking up. The figure is gone before a light is found.
Something slides under a closed door — a piece of paper, blank on both sides.
A voice begins whispering from inside the wall. Pressing an ear against it reveals the sound of breathing.
A window breaks from the inside, without visible cause, the glass falling outward.
A door that the party passed through is found, on return, to not exist. The wall is smooth and unbroken.
An item belonging to someone absent appears in the building — with no explanation for how it got there.
A figure seen from behind at the end of a corridor turns to reveal a featureless face.
The building's architecture is wrong — a room has no exit, a hallway is too short for the exterior dimensions.
An item belonging to someone dead is found where they would habitually keep it — warm, as if recently held.
A full-length apparition of a person appears, maintains eye contact, and walks through a wall.
The lights dim in one room every time a specific name is spoken.
The building's exterior, seen from inside, does not match the outside view.
A sound of a large crowd — talking, laughing — from outside a window that opens onto an empty field.
A footprint appears in dust in a sealed room, facing the door, as if someone stood waiting.
An empty rocking chair moves. Ceases when observed directly. Continues peripherally.
Someone's reflection in a window blinks at a different moment than they do.
A staircase that wasn't there before is found at the back of a closet.
Furniture moves — not abruptly, but audibly, across a room above, in patterns suggesting habitation.
The smell of a specific person — perfume, tobacco, sweat — too strong for anyone present to be wearing it.
A child's handprints appear on a lower section of wall in an area that has been painted over many times.
A specific door will not open regardless of force applied. There is nothing locking it.
The sound of a heartbeat — slow, massive — beneath the floor, felt more than heard.
An apparition sits at the dining table, motionless. When the lights are raised fully, it is gone. The chair is warm.
A diary or journal found in the building documents events that have just happened to the party.
An old voicemail or recording contains, in a moment of silence, the sound of breathing.
The building's floor plan does not correspond to the map drawn of it — there is a room on the map that cannot be found.
Something moves in the attic continuously for an extended period and stops when someone calls out.
A section of wall sounds hollow. Opening it reveals a sealed space containing something that should not be there.
An item the party placed in a specific location is found elsewhere. Surveillance shows the item moving on its own.
A formerly unknown figure appears in the background of every photograph taken in the building.
A figure seen in a mirror does not match the person standing in front of it — same body, different face.
All the clocks stop at the moment something significant happens, and do not restart.
A message is found written on the inside of a sealed container that has clearly never been opened.
Two party members, separated, both hear the same conversation from opposite ends of the building.
A fire that has been out for years is found burning in a fireplace. The fuel is old.
The building appears different at night than during the day — not dramatically, but verifiably.
An apparition reenacts a past event in the building, walking through the party as though they are not there.
A name is found scratched into the inside of a window frame — over and over, in different sizes and pressures.
An infant is heard crying from a room that contains only old, broken furniture.
A figure appears at the end of the bed of a sleeping party member. Another member wakes and sees it too.
A door opens and behind it is not the room that should be there.
Something is under the floorboards. It is not structural. It is not pipes. It moves.
A party member begins remembering events from the building's past that they should have no knowledge of.
A shadow passes along the wall in a direction opposite to where any light source is positioned.
A party member falls asleep and cannot be woken for a period far longer than sleep.
The exterior of the building, seen from outside after dark, shows all rooms lit. Inside, the lights are all off.
Someone in the party reports a conversation they had last night. No one else was present. No one remembers them leaving.
A sealed room, entered for the first time, contains furniture arranged as it was decades ago, undisturbed, with current-day objects placed among the antiques.
A party member's name is carved into a piece of furniture that is clearly decades old.
A presence follows a specific party member through the building — detected by the others but not perceived by the member themselves.
The building contains a room at the end of a hallway that is not on the floor plan and that everyone has avoided without noticing they were doing so.
A figure appears at the window of a room the party is in, staring. When the party moves toward it, the figure moves with the reflection, not the viewer.
All organic material in a specific room — food, plants, a dead insect on the sill — is found reduced to the same state of decay.
A voice on a recording, listened to carefully, is speaking to whoever is currently in the room — using the party's names.
Something is physically holding a door closed. The force applied cannot be matched. It releases after exactly one minute.
A series of events that the party experienced are described in a document clearly written before they arrived.
A figure appears behind a party member in a mirror, places its hands on their shoulders, and smiles. The party member feels nothing.
A room is found with its furniture arranged in a specific pattern centered on one spot on the floor. Below that spot, something has been buried.
Every time a party member reaches for a specific object, something pulls it away before they touch it.
A figure enters the building through a locked door without opening it and walks to the center of the room and stands there, facing away.
A party member wakes with marks on their body that were not there before sleep — not threatening, but deliberate. Like they were measured.
The building is found, on the second night, to contain a room that exists only at night. It is furnished for a specific person. That person is dead.
The entity responsible for everything that has been happening in the building makes itself visible, fully and clearly, in the center of the main room, and waits.
